entries.definition:
كتاب القاف

قدف - قدم

(قدم) القاف والدال والميم أصلٌ صحيحٌ يدلُّ على سَبْق ورَعْف ثم يفرَّع منه ما يقاربُه: يقولون: القِدَم: خلاف الحُدوث. ويقال: شيءٌ قديم، إذا كان زمانُهُ سالفاً. وأصله قولُهم: مضَى فُلاَنٌ قُدُماً: لم يعرِّج ولم ينْثَنِ. وربما صغَّروا القُدّام قُدَيْدِيماً وقُدَيْدِيمةٌ. قال القُطاميُّ:

قُدَيديمَةُ التَّحريبِ والحِلْم إنَّني

أرى غَفَلات العيشِ قَبْلَ التَّجَارِبِ

ويقال: ضُرِب فرَكِب مقاديمَه، إذا وقَع على وجهه. وقادِمَة الرَّحْلِ: خلاف آخِرَته. والقادمة من أطْبَاء النَّاقة: ما وَلِيَ السُّرَّة. ولفلانٍ قدمُ صدقٍ، أي شيءٌ متقدِّم من أثَر حسَن.

ومن الباب: قَدِم من سفره قُدوماً، وأقْدَم على الشيء إقداماً.

قال ابن دريد : وقادِمُ الإنسان: رأسُه، والجمع قوادم. قال: ولا يكادون يتكلَّمون بالواحد. وقوادم الطَّير: مقاديم الرِّيش، عشرٌ في كلِّ جَناحٍ، الواحدةُ

essence of purity, the spirit of holiness, interpreted as 1 epithet of the Archangel Gabriel (q.v.) (16:102) نَزَّلَهُ رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ مِنْ رَبِّكَ بِالْحَقِّ the Holy Spirit has brought it with the Truth (q.v.) from your Lord 2 the spirit that God sent to the Virgin Mary as a substitute for procreation through a father, or the power by which Jesus raised the dead (2:253) وَآتَيْنَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّنَاتِ وَأَيَّدْنَاهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ We gave Jesus, son of Mary, Our clear signs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit.
الْقُدُّوس ʾal-quddūs [n.] [an attribute of God] the Holy One (59:23) الْمَلِكُ الْقُدُّوسُ السَّلَامُ the King, the Holy One, Source of Peace.

مُقَدَّس muqaddas [quasi-pass. part.; fem مُقَدَّسَة muqaddasatun] blessed, made sacred (20:12) فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوَادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى so take off both your shoes; you are in the sacred valley, Ṭuwā.
entries.definition_english:
essence of purity, the spirit of holiness, interpreted as 1 epithet of the Archangel Gabriel (q.v.) (16:102) نَزَّلَهُ رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ مِنْ رَبِّكَ بِالْحَقِّ the Holy Spirit has brought it with the Truth (q.v.) from your Lord 2 the spirit that God sent to the Virgin Mary as a substitute for procreation through a father, or the power by which Jesus raised the dead (2:253) وَآتَيْنَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّنَاتِ وَأَيَّدْنَاهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ We gave Jesus, son of Mary, Our clear signs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit.
الْقُدُّوس ʾal-quddūs [n.] [an attribute of God] the Holy One (59:23) الْمَلِكُ الْقُدُّوسُ السَّلَامُ the King, the Holy One, Source of Peace.

مُقَدَّس muqaddas [quasi-pass. part.; fem مُقَدَّسَة muqaddasatun] blessed, made sacred (20:12) فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوَادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى so take off both your shoes; you are in the sacred valley, Ṭuwā.

entries.definition:
q–d–m foot; position, rank, leader, to lead; to come, to arrive; front, to advance, fore, in the front; brave, courageous; to precede, to be old, ancient, eternal; to submit. Of this root, 10 forms occur 48 times in the Qur’an: قَدِم qadima once; يَقْدُم yaqdum once; قَدَّم qaddama 27 times; تَقَدَّم taqaddama twice; تَسْتَقْدِمُونَ tastaqdimūn four times; قَدَم qadam twice; أَقْدَام ʾaqdām six times; قَدِيم qadīm three times; أَقْدَمُونَ ʾaqdamūn once and مُسْتَقْدِمِين mustaqdimīn once.
قَدِم qadima a [v. intrans.] to come to, to apply oneself to, to turn to (25:23) وَقَدِمْنَا إِلَى مَا عَمِلُوا مِنْ عَمَلٍ فَجَعَلْنَاهُ هَبَاءً مَنْثُورًا and We will turn to whatever [good] deeds they have done and scatter them like dust.

يَقْدُم yaqdum [imperf. of v. قَدَم qadama, trans.] to precede (11:98) يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ he will be at the forefront of his people on the Day of Resurrection.

قَدَّم qaddama [v. II, trans.] 1 to advance, to put forward, to offer (58:13) أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ have you found it burdensome to give alms before your private consultation [with the Prophet]? 2 [with an elliptical object] to work for, to show concern, to provide (89:24) يَقُولُ يَا لَيْتَنِي قَدَّمْتُ لِحَيَاتِي he will say, ‘Would that I had provided for my life [here]!’ 3 to offer, to make

744
entries.definition_english:
q–d–m foot; position, rank, leader, to lead; to come, to arrive; front, to advance, fore, in the front; brave, courageous; to precede, to be old, ancient, eternal; to submit. Of this root, 10 forms occur 48 times in the Qur’an: قَدِم qadima once; يَقْدُم yaqdum once; قَدَّم qaddama 27 times; تَقَدَّم taqaddama twice; تَسْتَقْدِمُونَ tastaqdimūn four times; قَدَم qadam twice; أَقْدَام ʾaqdām six times; قَدِيم qadīm three times; أَقْدَمُونَ ʾaqdamūn once and مُسْتَقْدِمِين mustaqdimīn once.
قَدِم qadima a [v. intrans.] to come to, to apply oneself to, to turn to (25:23) وَقَدِمْنَا إِلَى مَا عَمِلُوا مِنْ عَمَلٍ فَجَعَلْنَاهُ هَبَاءً مَنْثُورًا and We will turn to whatever [good] deeds they have done and scatter them like dust.

يَقْدُم yaqdum [imperf. of v. قَدَم qadama, trans.] to precede (11:98) يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ he will be at the forefront of his people on the Day of Resurrection.

قَدَّم qaddama [v. II, trans.] 1 to advance, to put forward, to offer (58:13) أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ have you found it burdensome to give alms before your private consultation [with the Prophet]? 2 [with an elliptical object] to work for, to show concern, to provide (89:24) يَقُولُ يَا لَيْتَنِي قَدَّمْتُ لِحَيَاتِي he will say, ‘Would that I had provided for my life [here]!’ 3 to offer, to make

744

entries.definition:
available, to bring upon (38:60) أَنْتُمْ قَدَّمْتُمُوهُ لَنَا it was you who brought this on us 4 to tempt, to entice, to allure, to beguile (5:80) لَبِئْسَ مَا قَدَّمَتْ لَهُمْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ أَنْ سَخِطَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ how vile is what their souls have beguiled them into! God is wroth with them as a result 5 to commit, to perpetrate, to incur (3:182) ذَلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ this is on account of what you have committed with your own hands: God is never unjust to [His] servants.
تَقَدَّم taqaddama [v. V, intrans.] 1 to go before (48:2) لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ that God may forgive you those of your sins that are past [lit. long gone] and those yet to come 2 to go forward, to advance (74:37) لِمَنْ شَاءَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوْ يَتَأَخَّرَ to those of you who choose to go ahead or lag behind.

يَسْتَقْدِم yastaqdim [imperf. of v. X, trans.] to seek to advance, to seek to bring forward (16:61) فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ and when their time comes they are unable to put it off for an hour nor [can they] bring it forward [for an hour].

قَدَم qadam [n.; pl. أَقْدَام ʾaqdām] foot, foothold *(16:94) فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا lest you sin after having attained to faith [lit. lest a foot should slip after it has been firmly placed]; *(10:2) قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ in good standing (or, on a sure footing) with their Lord; *(55:41) فَيُؤْخَذُ بِالنَّوَاصِي وَالْأَقْدَامِ and will be seized by the forelocks and the feet; *(41:29) فَنَجْعَلَهُمَا تَحْتَ أَقْدَامِنَا so we may humiliate them [lit. so we may put them under our feet]; *(8:11) وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ الْأَقْدَامَ and with which to strengthen the resolution [lit. and with which to make feet firm].

قَدِيم qadīm [quasi-act. part.] 1 ancient (46:11) هَذَا إِفْكٌ قَدِيمٌ and, since they will not be guided by it, they say, ‘This is an ancient fabrication’ 2 old, shrivelled (36:39) حَتَّى عَادَ كَالْعُرْجُونِ الْقَدِيمِ and the moon, We have determined it by stations until it becomes like a shrivelled, old date-palm stalk 3 customary, familiar, same old (thing) (12:95) إِنَّكَ لَفِي ضَلَالِكَ الْقَدِيمِ they said, ‘By God, you are still in your old delusions!’

أَقْدَمُون ʾaqdamūn [pl. of elat. أَقْدَم ʾaqdam] older/oldest, more/most ancient *(26:76) آبَاؤُكُمُ الْأَقْدَمُونَ your forefathers, your ancestors, your predecessors.

745
